Residuals from a fitted milt model

Source: R/model-interface.R, R/model-predict.R
milt_residuals.Rd

Returns the vector of training residuals (actual minus fitted) from a fitted model. Useful for residual diagnostics and assumption checking.

Usage

milt_residuals(model, ...)

milt_residuals(model, ...)

Arguments

model

A fitted MiltModel.

...

Additional arguments forwarded to the backend's residuals() method.

Value

A numeric vector of residuals (actual minus fitted).

A numeric vector of residuals with the same length as the training series.

See also

milt_predict(), milt_diagnose()

Other model: milt_backtest(), milt_compare(), milt_cv(), milt_ensemble(), milt_fit(), milt_forecast(), milt_local_model(), milt_model(), milt_predict(), milt_refit()

Other model: milt_backtest(), milt_compare(), milt_cv(), milt_ensemble(), milt_fit(), milt_forecast(), milt_local_model(), milt_model(), milt_predict(), milt_refit()

Examples

# \donttest{
s   <- milt_series(AirPassengers)
m   <- milt_model("naive") |> milt_fit(s)
#> Fitting <MiltNaive> model…
#> Done in 0s.
res <- milt_residuals(m)
mean(res, na.rm = TRUE)  # close to 0 for well-specified models
#> [1] 2.237762
# }
